problem.title: Populating Next Right Pointers in Each Node
problem.description: You are given a perfect binary tree where all leaves are on the same level, and every parent has two children. Populate each next pointer to point to its next right node. If there is no next right node, the next pointer should be set to NULL.
assumptions: Perfect binary tree (all leaves at same level, all non-leaves have two children).
O(1) extra space using existing next pointers.

example.explanation: The next pointers connect nodes at the same level.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Level Order Traversal
solutions.description: Use BFS to connect nodes at the same level.

complexity.space: O(w) where w is the maximum width

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Using Existing Next Pointers
solutions.description: Exploit the perfect-tree property: once a level's next pointers are set, use them to traverse that level and wire up the children below in O(1) space. For each parent, connect its left child to its right child, and its right child to the left child of the parent's next node.
solutions.code_python:
from typing import Optional

def connect_optimal(root: Optional[object]) -> Optional[object]:
    if not root:
        return None

    leftmost = root
    while leftmost.left:  # stop at leaf level
        current = leftmost
        while current:
            # Connect left child to right child
            current.left.next = current.right
            # Connect right child to the left child of the next parent
            if current.next:
                current.right.next = current.next.left
            current = current.next
        leftmost = leftmost.left

    return root
